grant_title: State of the Art and Challenges for Offshore Integrated Multi-Trophic Aquaculture (IMTA)
geographic_restrictions: Primarily focused on offshore and open ocean environments, with case studies from Germany, North Sea, German Bight, and other international locations. Applicants must demonstrate capacity to operate in exposed marine environments.
project_area: Marine aquaculture, offshore integrated multi-trophic aquaculture (IMTA), sustainable food production, environmental remediation, and socio-economic analysis of marine resource utilization.
demographic_requirements: No specific demographic requirements mentioned. Focus is on institutional and organizational capacity to conduct research or implement projects in offshore IMTA systems.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include research institutions, universities, government agencies, private sector entities, non-profits, and international organizations with demonstrated expertise in marine aquaculture, offshore systems, or related fields. Projects must align with the goals of advancing offshore IMTA, including biological feasibility, technological innovation, environmental sustainability, and socio-economic viability. Collaboration across disciplines and stakeholders is encouraged.
